title: terrellia × macounii
intro: Terrellia × macounii, also known as Macoun's aster, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant in the daisy family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds that can help soothe skin irritations and wounds. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, and it has been used in herbal preparations such as tinctures and salves to aid in the treatment of various skin conditions. However, due to its rarity and endangered status, harvesting and use of the plant are strictly regulated to ensure its survival.
